name: Vertical Timeline Split
description: A two-column section with a sticky narrative on the left and a vertical timeline of dated events on the right, designed for company histories, roadmap reveals, and milestone retrospectives.

descriptions.descriptive: Asymmetric split with a sticky narrative column on the left and a vertical timeline on the right. Each timeline entry combines a monospaced marker, an optional metric chip, a heading, and a short body. A single hairline runs the full height of the timeline, intersected by ringed nodes at every event. Generous vertical spacing creates a calm reading rhythm without the heaviness of cards.
descriptions.usage: Use to walk readers through an ordered sequence of moments — company milestones, product changelog highlights, regulatory timelines, or a roadmap reveal. Best when the reader benefits from seeing the full arc at a glance while focusing on one event at a time. Skip when events are unordered or when the dataset is large enough to warrant a real archive page.
descriptions.audienceFit: Resonates with B2B audiences who care about track record — investors, enterprise procurement teams, and partner sales. Fits established service firms, scaling SaaS, and craft-led brands with a story worth slowing down for. Less suited to consumer ecommerce or campaign landing pages, where dense feature cards convert faster than narrative.
